title: Generative diffusion machine learning for reservoir simulation model history matching
abstract: A method for generating history matched grid data (320). The method includes obtaining observed production data (302) and obtaining latent grid data (312). The method further includes processing the observed production data (302) to form observed latent production data (310) and generating denoised latent grid data (316) by removing noise from the latent grid data (312) using a diffusion model (314) guided by the observed latent production data (310) and a time encoding (315). The method further includes generating history matched grid data (320) by decoding the denoised latent grid data (316) and optimizing production of a well using the history matched grid data (320).
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: obtaining observed production data (302); obtaining latent grid data (312); processing the observed production data (302) to form observed latent production data (310); generating denoised latent grid data (316) by removing noise from the latent grid data (312) using a diffusion model (314) guided by the observed latent production data (310) and a time encoding (315); generating history matched grid data (320) by decoding the denoised latent grid data (316); and optimizing production of a well using the history matched grid data (320). 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising forecasting future production of a hydrocarbon reservoir described, at least in part, by the history matched grid data (320). 3. The method of claim 1 or claim 2, further comprising planning a wellbore (102) to penetrate a hydrocarbon reservoir based on the history matched grid data (320), wherein the planned wellbore (102) comprises a planned wellbore path. 4. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising generating the time encoding using a positional encoding method, wherein the time encoding comprises at least one time encoding vector (806). 5. The method of any preceding claim, wherein the history matched grid data (320) comprises a three-dimensional porosity grid and a three-dimensional permeability grid for a subsurface. 6. The method of any preceding claim, wherein the observed production data (302) is processed using an encoder (306) of a first autoencoder (305) to form the observed latent production data (310). 7. The method of any preceding claim, wherein the denoised latent grid data (316) is decoded using a decoder (318) of a second autoencoder (307). 8. The method of claim 6, wherein the encoder (306) comprises a neural network. 9. The method of claim 7, wherein the decoder (318) comprises a convolutional neural network. 10. The method of claim any preceding claim, wherein the latent grid data (312) is obtained by randomly generating grid data in a latent grid data space. 11. A system, comprising: a first autoencoder (305) comprising a first encoder (306) and a first decoder (317); a second autoencoder (307) comprising a second encoder (308) and a second decoder (318); a diffusion model (314); a time encoding (315) comprising at least one time encoding vector (806); and a computer (1102) comprising one or more computer processors (1105) and a non- transitory computer-readable medium (1106), the computer (1102) configured to: receive observed production data (302); obtain latent grid data (312); process the observed production data (302) with the first encoder (306) to form observed latent production data (310); generate denoised latent grid data (316) by removing noise from the latent grid data (312) using the diffusion model (314) guided by the observed latent production data (310) and the time encoding (315); generate history matched grid data (320) by decoding the denoised latent grid data (316) using the second decoder (318); and optimize production of a well using the history matched grid data (320). 12. The system of claim 11, further comprising a wellbore planning system (190) to plan a wellbore (102) to penetrate a hydrocarbon reservoir based on the history matched grid data (320), wherein the planned wellbore (102) comprises a planned wellbore path. 13. The system of claim 12, further comprising a drilling system configured to drill the wellbore (102) guided by the planned wellbore path. 14. The system of any one of claims 11 to 13, wherein the history matched grid data (320) comprises a three-dimensional porosity grid and a three-dimensional permeability grid for a subsurface. 15. The system of any one of claims 11 to 14 claim, wherein the first encoder (306) comprises a first neural network and the first decoder (317) comprises a second neural network. 16. The system of any one of claims 11 to 15 claim, wherein the second encoder (308) comprises a first convolutional neural network and the second decoder (318) comprises a second convolutional neural network. 17. The system of any one of claims 1 to 16, wherein the latent grid data (312) is obtained by randomly generating grid data in a latent grid data space. 18. A non-transitory computer readable medium (1106) storing instructions executable by a computer processor (1105), the instructions comprising functionality for: receiving observed production data (302); obtaining latent grid data (312); processing the observed production data (302) with a first encoder (306) to form observed latent production data (310); generating denoised latent grid data (316) by removing noise from the latent grid data (312) using a diffusion model (314) guided by the observed latent production data (310) and a time encoding (315); generating history matched grid data (320) by decoding the denoised latent grid data (316) using a first decoder (318); and optimizing production of a well using the history matched grid data (320). 19. The non-transitory computer readable medium (1106) of claim 18, wherein the instructions further comprise functionality for planning a wellbore (102) to penetrate a hydrocarbon reservoir based on the history matched grid data (320), wherein the planned wellbore (102) comprises a planned wellbore path. 0. The non-transitory computer readable medium (1106) of claim 18 or claim 19, wherein the latent grid data (312) is obtained by randomly generating grid data in a latent grid data space.
